message:
fix: ExpensiveReplay safety envelope cap and GradedScope close semantics

Two related correctness fixes:

1. messaging/policy.rs — compile_safety_envelope did not cap the
   max_delivery_class for ExpensiveReplay workloads, allowing the
   compiler to assign a delivery class above ForensicReplayable.
   Fix: clamp max_delivery_class to ForensicReplayable when the
   service class is ExpensiveReplay.  Test assertion added to the
   existing compiler_maps_expensive_replay_to_forensic_lane test.

2. obligation/graded.rs — GradedScope::close() previously left
   self.closed = false on error so that Drop would fire the leak
   panic.  However, when a caller explicitly calls close() and
   receives the Err, they are already aware of the outstanding
   obligations — double-panicking in Drop is counterproductive and
   can mask the actual error in test/production contexts.
   Fix: set self.closed = true before returning Err, reserving the
   Drop panic for the implicit-drop case where close() was never
   called.  Test updated from should_panic to a normal assertion
   on the error's outstanding count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
